incidents.text: OpenAI GPT-4 system prompt extraction toolkit — systematic prompt leakage Security researchers published a comprehensive toolkit for extracting system prompts from GPT-4 and other production LLMs. The toolkit combined multi-turn conversation steering, encoding tricks (Base64, ROT13), and role-play scenarios to reliably extract complete system prompts from deployed applications. The research demonstrated that prompt confidentiality is fundamentally broken as a security control. System prompt confidentiality proven unreliable; IP exposure for prompt-dependent applications; forces architectural redesign away from prompt-as-security
incidents.notes: TP. System prompt extraction outcome → LLM07. Mechanism uses 'encoding tricks (Base64, ROT13)' and 'role-play scenarios' = jailbreak/injection vectors → LLM01. LLM07 exclusions allow this co-label: 'Prompt injection as the extraction mechanism (→ LLM01 for the vector)' — vector LLM01, outcome LLM07.

incidents.text: SPE-LLM: System Prompt Extraction Attacks and Defenses Systematic framework for evaluating prompt-extraction attacks against state-of-the-art LLMs, releasing adversarial queries that reliably extract system prompts and benchmarking defenses.
incidents.notes: TP. Same pattern as INC-04067: 'adversarial queries' = injection vector (LLM01), outcome is system prompt extraction (LLM07). Co-label.
